threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You know you’re new when you queue for a coffee at Shacklewell Lane like it’s a normal cafe, not just a trendy hotspot. Instant side-eye from regulars.
Heard a guy on the N68 talking about the 'secret curry spot' near the market that only opens after midnight. Every night bus has its own stories, doesn’t it?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a guy repairing a bike while sharing a plate of döner with his dog at the bus stop. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a currywurst at 3 AM after a night out and watch the sun come up over the Spree. It's a vibe like no other.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a true local knows to dodge the Civic Center BART exit on Fridays—loud bangs and smoke lead to chaos, but tourists just stare.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus today with a guy singing opera to calm the anxious crowd after a delay. Only in SF do we turn transit woes into a concert.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists flock to Park Güell but miss the little plazas like Plaça de la Vila, where locals actually hang out over a vermut.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they ask for directions with a map in front of them, while locals just glance at their phones.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You pause at a crossing for more than three seconds, thinking you have the right of way. Locals are already halfway down the street.
Caught a late 59 in Brixton and overheard two mates arguing about who snored loudest on the last bus home. Classic night bus banter!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I was really living here? Hearing an opera performance wafting out of a window while a dog barked along on the street.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you sip coffee while a giant bear mural silently watches the morning hustle; it’s like having a guardian for your caffeine fix.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they’re local when they complain about the bus delays while casually dodging the dog on the tracks at Civic Center.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 47 at Civic Center just as the dog jumped off the tracks—nothing like a canine interruption during rush hour to capture SF's charm.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the fountains in Gràcia are for their Instagram shots, but locals dodge the splashes while enjoying a quiet beer nearby.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their endless selfies in front of Park Güell—locals just go for a stroll and avoid that selfie spot altogether.
